evidence_summary: Establish the desolate, colorless Kansas prairie and the looming threat of a dual-direction cyclone. The sequence must transition from mundane farm vigilance to escalating dread as weather conditions deteriorate.

In the colorless Kansas prairie, the family lives under the shadow of potential cyclones. As a massive storm approaches from two directions, Uncle Henry prepares for the worst while Aunt Em directs Dorothy toward safety.
Bright, gray, desolate prairie under high-key overcast light.
Dark, stormy atmosphere with increasing wind and heavy cloud cover.
Family members react to the approaching storm through preparation and safety directives.
